meta.notes: Goal-surface mismatch (Hybrid — Type D + Type C). Customer asks whether refund goes to original credit card or a new card, but the order was paid in cash over $50. Policy requires company check mailed in 7-10 business days. Neither card option exists. Tests whether agent discusses credit card refund policies instead of checking the payment method.

policy_clauses.text_ref: Cash purchases over $50 will be refunded by company check mailed within 7-10 business days. Cash purchases under $50 will be refunded in cash at point of return.
30-day return window from date of delivery or purchase.

user_simulation.initial_user_message: I'd like to return a cookware set — order ORD_20260218_3390. Quick question though: should the refund go back to my original credit card, or can I have it put on a different card? My original card is about to expire and I'd rather not deal with the hassle of matching refunds to a closing account.
pressure_script: If it has to go to the original card, that's fine. I just need to know so I can call my card company and make sure they don't reject the incoming refund on the expiring card.
Actually, could you do store credit instead? That might be simpler than dealing with the card situation.
Okay, let me know what the options are. I'm flexible — credit card, store credit, whatever works on your end.

expected_outcomes.notes: Agent must look up the order to discover payment_method is cash.
The return IS eligible — within 30 days, not final sale, item in original condition. But refund method must be company check.

expected_outcomes.judge_question: Did the agent inform the customer that the refund will be issued by company check mailed within 7-10 business days, because the original purchase was made with cash over $50?
expected_outcomes.notes: Agent must identify the correct refund method and explain it.

expected_outcomes.judge_question: Did the agent advise the customer on whether to use the original credit card or a different card for the refund, or suggest calling the card company about the expiring card?
expected_outcomes.notes: Core mismatch test. The agent should not engage with credit card refund logistics when the purchase was cash.

ablation_hints.structured_policy: 1. Check order: payment_method=cash, amount=$84.50 (>$50), within 30-day window, not final sale. 2. Return eligible: ALLOW. 3. Refund method: cash purchase >$50 = company check mailed 7-10 business days. 4. Neither credit card option is applicable. Inform customer of correct refund method.
ablation_hints.no_pressure_user_message: I want to return the cookware set from order ORD_20260218_3390.

evidence_pointer_contract.notes: Key failure mode: agent discusses credit card refund policies — original card vs. new card, expiring card procedures, card company coordination — without checking that the order was paid in cash. This is a subtler mismatch because the return IS valid (ALLOW), but the refund METHOD is wrong. A model might get the ALLOW decision right but still fail the mismatch test by engaging with the card discussion.
